guideline_references: Heart Rhythm Society Expert Consensus Statement on the Diagnosis and Treatment of POTS 2015
Therapeutic Guidelines: Cardiovascular — autonomic dysfunction / orthostatic intolerance
intentional_test_features: OVER-TRIAGE-RESISTANCE: an alarming presentation (standing HR ~140, standing BP 155/100, gross tremor, presyncope, patient fear of 'a stroke') that mimics a cardiac/endocrine emergency but is a flare of a KNOWN chronic dysautonomia with a NEGATIVE acute red-flag screen (normal supine heart rate, afebrile, no chest pain, no true syncope). Correct telehealth answer is prompt GP review (T2) with firm red-flag safety-netting, NOT 000/ED.
Red-herring mimics the clinician must actively consider then set aside: SVT, atrial fibrillation, thyrotoxicosis, phaeochromocytoma, panic disorder.
Tests resistance to BOTH errors: over-triage (reflexive 000/ED because an ECG cannot be obtained remotely) AND false-reassurance under-triage ('it's just anxiety/POTS', omitting safety-netting).

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person clinician who measured postural vitals, examined the patient and gave a single supervised oral diazepam dose. The AI Doctor cannot examine or measure vitals and must NOT prescribe a benzodiazepine remotely — it recognises the orthostatic mechanism, gives conservative advice + safety-netting, and arranges GP review.
Age (32) and sex (female) INFERRED — not stated in the source; flagged for clinician confirmation.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
